cap_space_post_fa: TBD — new signings (Edwards 4yr/$61M, Etienne 4yr/$52M, Wright 4yr/$14M, Fant 2yr, Ridgeway 2yr/$6.2M) will reduce remaining space significantly; estimated low single digits or negative requiring further restructures

key_departures.impact: Davis was the defensive captain and engine of the Saints' LB corps. His departure to the Jets on a 2yr/$22M deal is a significant loss. New Orleans had no other established starting-caliber LB behind him.

Taylor was the Saints' best cornerback — versatile inside-outside player with 10-plus pass defenses in each of his four NFL seasons. His departure at $20M/year to Tennessee is a massive loss. New Orleans has no established CB1 replacement on roster.

key_departures.new_team: TBD/unsigned — testing free market as of March 10
key_departures.impact: Jordan recorded 10.5 sacks in 2025 (his most since 2021) and was the Saints' primary pass rusher. His contract voided at the start of the new league year. The Saints will absorb $18.76M in dead money. He intends to play a 16th season but no deal is yet in place with New Orleans or another team. His departure leaves a serious EDGE hole — Chase Young is now the de facto starter but has injury history.

key_departures.new_team: Seattle Seahawks (re-signed, was previously traded mid-2025)
key_departures.impact: Shaheed was traded to Seattle at the 2025 trade deadline for a 4th and 5th round pick. The Saints already processed this departure mid-season. WR loss was already reflected in pre-FA need picture. New Orleans received draft compensation (2026 4th and 5th round picks).
key_departures.notes: Departure was a mid-2025 trade, not a 2026 FA departure. Already baked into pre-FA needs.

key_departures.impact: Hill's contract voided at the new league year. At 36 with significant injury history, retirement is expected. His unique gadget/TE hybrid role is difficult to replicate but he had a limited role in 2025. Loss adds urgency to TE depth, partially addressed by Fant signing.
Moreau's contract voided with the new league year. He was a reliable blocking TE and receiving option behind Juwan Johnson. His departure further thins the TE room ahead of the Fant signing.
Radunz served as a one-year stopgap at guard/tackle. His departure leaves room for Edwards to immediately step in. Given the Edwards signing, this departure creates no new need.
Fortner was the starting center. His departure could leave a hole at center that the Edwards signing (guard) does not address. Center remains an unresolved need.

contract.guaranteed: TBD — reported at $52M total; guarantees not yet fully disclosed, estimated ~$28-32M based on market comps

signings.analysis: Etienne, 27, is a Louisiana native returning home on a 4yr/$52M deal that makes him one of the highest-paid RBs in the league. He rushed for 1,107 yards and 7 TDs in 2025 and added 36 receptions for 292 yards and 6 receiving TDs. The 4-year commitment is real investment — not a bridge deal. Etienne immediately becomes RB1. Alvin Kamara (30, restructured, declining) is now a candidate for release/trade. RB need is fully addressed for the foreseeable future. Draft pick not needed at RB.

contract.guaranteed: TBD — guarantees not yet fully disclosed; AAV of $15.25M is top-of-market; estimated ~$30-35M based on market comps

signings.analysis: Edwards, 28, was considered the top available guard on the market. The 4yr/$61M deal at $15.25M/year is a genuine investment in the interior OL. He slots immediately into the starting LG spot — a position that had cycled through a series of one-year deals for years. This fully addresses the LG need. However, center (Luke Fortner departed) remains unaddressed. IOL partially addressed — LG is solved, C is still a need.

Wright replaces Kai Kroeger, who averaged only 37.3 net yards per punt (among the league's worst). The Saints invested in the punter position with a 4yr/$14M deal ($8M guaranteed) — an unusually strong commitment that signals they view punting as a genuine need rather than an afterthought. Punter position now fully addressed and off the draft board.

contract.total_value: TBD — no financial terms reported as of March 10
contract.aav: TBD — market context: depth-tier TE; likely $5-8M/yr based on his prior deal history

signings.analysis: Fant, a former first-round pick, joins Juwan Johnson (returning) to form the Saints' TE room after Hill and Moreau's contracts voided. He had 34 catches, 288 yards, and 3 TDs with Cincinnati in 2025 but also lost 3 fumbles for TDs. Fant brings superior run-blocking to complement Johnson (who grades poorly as a blocker). The 2-year deal at depth-level money signals he is a complementary player, not a true TE1. TE is partially addressed — Juwan Johnson remains TE1 but the room is thinner than ideal and could benefit from a draft pick.

signings.previous_team: Dallas Cowboys (acquired via Day 3 pick trade)
signings.analysis: Ridgeway was acquired for a Day 3 draft pick and is a depth-type interior lineman. He missed significant time in 2025 with an IR stint. The 2yr/$6.2M deal with only $1.65M guaranteed is low-commitment, rotation-level signing. Does not address any starter-level IDL need. Minimal impact on need picture.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Travis Etienne (4yr/$52M, $13M AAV) is a genuine RB1 investment at age 27. He had 1,107 rush yards and 13 total TDs in 2025. Alvin Kamara (30, restructured) is now a likely cut/trade candidate. RB is effectively off the board for the draft. Do not draft RB in rounds 1-4.

David Edwards (4yr/$61M) fully solves LG. However, starting C Luke Fortner departed and is unsigned elsewhere. The Saints have no established center on the roster. IOL remains a tier 2 need specifically at center — rounds 2-4 candidate or free agency addition.

Demario Davis (2yr/$22M) signed with the Jets. He was the defensive captain and the Saints' best LB. No replacement was signed. With no other established starter on the roster, LB escalates to tier 1 critical need — top draft priority at picks 8, or a mid-round target if addressed in a future FA signing.

Alontae Taylor (3yr/$60M, $42M guaranteed) signed with Tennessee — a massive loss. Taylor was the Saints' CB1 and arguably the top CB in this FA class. Paulson Adebo departed in the prior offseason as well. The Saints have lost homegrown CBs in back-to-back free agencies. No CB replacement has been signed. CB is now a critical tier 1 need alongside LB. Draft picks 8 or follow-on rounds 2-3.

Cameron Jordan (10.5 sacks in 2025) has voided his contract and is testing free agency. No deal is in place as of March 10. Chase Young (restructured) is the likely starter but has significant injury history. The Saints' pass rush is now thin heading into the draft. EDGE is a tier 1 need unless Jordan is re-signed. If Jordan signs elsewhere, this becomes a high-priority draft target.

Juwan Johnson (re-signed last offseason) returns as TE1, Noah Fant added on a 2-year depth deal. But Taysom Hill (likely retired) and Foster Moreau (voided) both left. Fant partially fills the gap but isn't a true starter. TE remains a tier 3 mid-round draft target (rounds 3-5).

No WR was signed in free agency. Rashid Shaheed was traded away mid-2025. The current WR room is Chris Olave, Devaughn Vele, and Mason Tipton. This is one of the weakest WR rooms in the NFC. WR remains a tier 1 critical need — elite WR prospect could be the right pick at No. 8 if LB/CB/EDGE are addressed in FA or via trade. Strong rounds 1-2 target.

Ridgeway signing (2yr/$6.2M, depth) adds rotation depth but doesn't address a starter-level IDL need. Carl Granderson and Chase Young are the primary starters. IDL remains a tier 3 mid-to-late round depth target.

key_additions.headline: Louisiana native and 1,107-yard rusher returns home to become the unquestioned Saints RB1, ending the Kamara era
Top guard on the market locks down the LG spot with a 4-year commitment, ending years of revolving-door interior OL signings
Saints upgrade a genuine weakness at punter, replacing Kroeger (37.3 net yards/punt, league bottom) with a long-term solution
Former first-round pick adds blocking and red-zone presence to the TE room as Hill and Moreau depart
Depth interior lineman adds rotation to the DL; low-cost, low-commitment depth move

key_losses.headline: Defensive captain and long-time anchor departs to reunite with the Jets, leaving a massive leadership and production void at LB
Saints' best CB and the top corner in this FA class signs a $20M/yr deal with Tennessee, creating a critical secondary hole
Franchise sack leader (132 career, 10.5 in 2025) hits free agency for the first time after 15 seasons; Saints absorb $18.76M in dead money
Fan-favorite gadget player's unique TE/QB hybrid role ends as contract voids; retirement expected at age 36
Starting center departs, leaving the pivot position unaddressed despite the Edwards signing at guard

notes: Cameron Jordan status: As of March 10, 2026 (legal tampering window Day 2), Jordan's contract has voided and he is testing free agency. No new deal with Saints or another team confirmed. EDGE need assessment assumes he departs unless re-signed.
Travis Etienne guaranteed money: Total of $52M confirmed by multiple sources; precise guaranteed breakdown not yet reported. Estimated in the $28-32M range based on RB market comps at $13M AAV.
David Edwards guaranteed money: Contract total of $61M confirmed; guaranteed split not yet reported. Estimated in the $30-35M range based on IOL market at $15.25M AAV.
Noah Fant contract value: Two-year deal confirmed by multiple sources (NFL Network, ESPN); no financial terms reported as of March 10.
Taysom Hill status: Contract voided with new league year. Retirement widely expected but not yet officially announced.
Foster Moreau status: Contract voided with new league year. Destination/status unknown.
Rashid Shaheed departure was a mid-2025 trade to Seattle (Nov. 4, 2025), not a 2026 free agency departure. Already reflected in pre-FA needs. Saints received 2026 4th and 5th round picks.
